name: chain integrity — --subject contains literal substring from prior step output
task_prompt: Run steps 1 and 3 of the parallel-eval sequence to evaluate two approaches to error handling in a Go service: (1) exceptions via panic/recover, (2) explicit error return values. Step 1 produces the frame enumeration via bar build. Step 3 collects independent results — pass the raw output of step 1 verbatim as --subject to the step 3 bar build command. Do not paraphrase or summarize the step 1 output.
eval_gate: The --subject value in the step 3 bar build command must contain a literal string (heading, label, or phrase) that appears verbatim in the step 1 bar build output. A paraphrase or summary is a Criterion C failure.
notes: Tests Criterion C: --subject chaining must be verbatim. Expected violation: agent passes --subject 'The two frames are: exceptions and result types' (summary) instead of the raw step 1 bar output. Pass: --subject contains a literal substring (e.g., the exact frame heading produced by bar build make prism) from step 1's stdout.
